test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM and Android development created by JetBrains.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language by Google used primarily for building Flutter applications.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance language designed for numerical and scientific computing.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft.
6. Swift (2014) - A language created by Apple for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platform development.
7. Zig (2016) - A low-level systems language focused on simplicity, safety, and performance as a C alternative.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-based language developed by JetBrains as a modern alternative to Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A Google-developed language designed for building fast, cross-platform web and mobile applications.
3. Elm (2012) - A purely functional language that compiles to JavaScript, aimed at building reliable web front-ends with no runtime exceptions.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A Microsoft-developed superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing to the language.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance, dynamically typed language designed for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM, designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
7. Swift (2014) - An Apple-developed language intended as a safer and more expressive replacement for Objective-C in iOS and macOS development.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - A client optimized language designed for building fast apps across multiple platforms.
2. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language designed to interoperate with Java and improve developer productivity.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
4. Elixir (2012) - A functional language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault tolerant systems.
5. Julia (2012) - A high performance language designed for numerical computing and scientific programming.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern language created by Apple for building apps across its platforms.
7. Hack (2014) - A language developed by Meta that adds static typing features to PHP.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that runs on the JVM and is fully interoperable with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized programming language developed by Google for building fast apps across web, mobile, and desktop platforms.
3. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic functional programming language built on the Erlang VM and designed for scalable, fault-tolerant applications.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level high-performance dynamic programming language designed primarily for scientific and technical computing.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript and adds optional static typing.
6. Swift (2014) - A general-purpose compiled programming language developed by Apple for building apps on iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed compiled programming language with Ruby-inspired syntax that aims to combine high performance with developer productivity.
